perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS, on the date hereof, pursuant to that certain Asset Purchase Agreement of even date herewith (the "Purchase Agreement"), by and among the Buyer, UniPay, Inc. (the "Seller") and the Stockholder, the Buyer is acquiring all of the assets and properties of the Seller used to provide or act as an electronic gateway between sellers of goods and services and processors of payments for such goods and services made via credit cards and debit cards, presently located at Seller's facility in Murphy, North Carolina (the "Business"), as a going concern;
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS, on the date hereof, pursuant to that certain agreement (the "Purchase Agreement"), by and among the Buyer, UniPay, Inc. (the "Seller") and the Stockholder, the Buyer is acquiring some assets of the Seller;
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'Asset Purchase Agreement of even date herewith', and 'all of the assets and properties of the Seller used to provide or act as an electronic gateway between sellers of goods and services and processors of payments for such goods and services made via credit cards and debit cards, presently located at Seller's facility in Murphy, North Carolina (the "Business"), as a going concern' and replacing them with 'some assets', the agreement becomes vague on what the business entails. This omission contradicts section 1(a)(i) and (ii) that state: (i) the business contacts, customers, suppliers, technology, know- how, trade secrets, marketing techniques, operating methods and other aspects of the Business have been of value to the Seller, and have provided the Seller (and will hereafter provide the Buyer) with substantial competitive advantage in the operation of the Business, and (ii) by virtue of its ownership and management of the Seller as its sole stockholder, the Stockholder has detailed knowledge of and possesses confidential information concerning the Business.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Stockholder hereby acknowledges and agrees that: (i) the business contacts, customers, suppliers, technology, know- how, trade secrets, marketing techniques, operating methods and other aspects of the Business have been of value to the Seller, and have provided the Seller (and will hereafter provide the Buyer) with substantial competitive advantage in the operation of the Business, and (ii) by virtue of its ownership and management of the Seller as its sole stockholder, the Stockholder has detailed knowledge of and possesses confidential information concerning the Business.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits key details about the assets being acquired, replacing "all of the assets and properties of the Seller used to provide or act as an electronic gateway between sellers of goods and services and processors of payments for such goods and services made via credit cards and debit cards, presently located at Seller's facility in Murphy, North Carolina (the "Business"), as a going concern" with "some assets". This introduces vagueness, directly contradicting the contradicted text which specifies that "the business contacts, customers, suppliers, technology, know- how, trade secrets, marketing techniques, operating methods and other aspects of the Business have been of value to the Seller" and that "the Stockholder has detailed knowledge of and possesses confidential information concerning the Business". Since the changed text states that only 'some assets' were acquired, and therefore, the "Business" as previously defined no longer exists, then the contradicted text becomes void, and creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (i) at any time from and after the date hereof, except to the extent required by law or in connection with any legal proceeding of which the Stockholder has theretofore given written notice to the Buyer, divulge, transmit or otherwise disclose or cause to be divulged, transmitted or otherwise disclosed, any business contacts, client or customer lists, technology, know-how, trade secrets, marketing techniques, contracts or other confidential or proprietary information of the Seller or the Buyer of whatever nature, whether existing on or prior to the date hereof or arising from and after the date hereof (provided, however, that for purposes hereof, information shall not be considered to be confidential or proprietary if (A) it is a matter of common knowledge or public record, (B) it is generally known in the industry, or (C) the Stockholder can demonstrate that such information was already known to the recipient thereof other than by reason of any breach of any obligation under this Agreement or any other confidentiality or non- disclosure agreement known to the Stockholder); and/or
perturbation.changed_text: (i) at any time from and after the date hereof, divulge, transmit or otherwise disclose or cause to be divulged, transmitted or otherwise disclosed, confidential information of the Seller or the Buyer; and/or
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'except to the extent required by law or in connection with any legal proceeding of which the Stockholder has theretofore given written notice to the Buyer, divulge, transmit or otherwise disclose or cause to be divulged, transmitted or otherwise disclosed, any business contacts, client or customer lists, technology, know-how, trade secrets, marketing techniques, contracts or other confidential or proprietary information of the Seller or the Buyer of whatever nature, whether existing on or prior to the date hereof or arising from and after the date hereof (provided, however, that for purposes hereof, information shall not be considered to be confidential or proprietary if (A) it is a matter of common knowledge or public record, (B) it is generally known in the industry, or (C) the Stockholder can demonstrate that such information was already known to the recipient thereof other than by reason of any breach of any obligation under this Agreement or any other confidentiality or non- disclosure agreement known to the Stockholder);' This makes the agreement overly broad, as it does not define what information is considered confidential anymore. This contradicts section 6 that states: 'It is acknowledged, understood and agreed that the restrictions contained in this Agreement (a) are made for good, valuable and adequate consideration received and to be received by the Stockholder, and (b) are reasonable and necessary, in terms of the time, geographic scope and nature of the restrictions, for the protection of the Buyer and the good will thereof.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is acknowledged, understood and agreed that the restrictions contained in this Agreement (a) are made for good, valuable and adequate consideration received and to be received by the Stockholder, and (b) are reasonable and necessary, in terms of the time, geographic scope and nature of the restrictions, for the protection of the Buyer and the good will thereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies exceptions to the confidentiality clause and defines what is not considered confidential. The changed text removes these exceptions and the definition of what is not confidential, making the confidentiality clause overly broad. This contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the restrictions in the agreement are reasonable and necessary. Without the exceptions and clear definition of confidentiality, the restrictions may be deemed unreasonable and unenforceable, leading to a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: (ii) at any time from the date hereof through and including the second (2nd) anniversary of the date hereof (the "Restrictive Period"), solicit, seek to hire, employ or retain (directly or indirectly, whether as an employee, consultant or otherwise) any person employed or retained by the Buyer at such time or within one (1) year prior to such solicitation, hiring, employment or retention, or otherwise materially and adversely interfere with the relationship between the Buyer and any such person, without the prior written consent of the Buyer in each instance; and/or
perturbation.changed_text: (ii) at any time from the date hereof, solicit, seek to hire, employ or retain any person employed or retained by the Buyer; and/or
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'through and including the second (2nd) anniversary of the date hereof (the "Restrictive Period"), (directly or indirectly, whether as an employee, consultant or otherwise) at such time or within one (1) year prior to such solicitation, hiring, employment or retention, or otherwise materially and adversely interfere with the relationship between the Buyer and any such person, without the prior written consent of the Buyer in each instance;' the time period is undefined, creating a contradiction in section 6 stating: 'It is acknowledged, understood and agreed that the restrictions contained in this Agreement (a) are made for good, valuable and adequate consideration received and to be received by the Stockholder, and (b) are reasonable and necessary, in terms of the time, geographic scope and nature of the restrictions, for the protection of the Buyer and the good will thereof.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is acknowledged, understood and agreed that the restrictions contained in this Agreement (a) are made for good, valuable and adequate consideration received and to be received by the Stockholder, and (b) are reasonable and necessary, in terms of the time, geographic scope and nature of the restrictions, for the protection of the Buyer and the good will thereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined a restrictive period of two years for soliciting employees. The changed text removes the 'through and including the second (2nd) anniversary of the date hereof (the "Restrictive Period"), (directly or indirectly, whether as an employee, consultant or otherwise) at such time or within one (1) year prior to such solicitation, hiring, employment or retention, or otherwise materially and adversely interfere with the relationship between the Buyer and any such person, without the prior written consent of the Buyer in each instance;' portion, effectively removing any time limit on the restriction. This directly contradicts the statement in the 'Contradicted Text' which asserts that the restrictions are reasonable in terms of time. Without a defined time period, the restriction becomes potentially perpetual and therefore unreasonable, creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (iii) at any time during the Restrictive Period, solicit, canvass or approach or endeavor to solicit, canvass or approach any person or entity which the Stockholder knows (or should have known) was provided with products or services of the Business by the Seller or the Stockholder at any time, for the purpose of offering services or products which compete with or are functionally similar to the services or products of the Business supplied by the Seller or the Stockholder.
perturbation.changed_text: (iii) at any time, solicit, canvass or approach any person or entity for the purpose of offering services or products.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'during the Restrictive Period, which the Stockholder knows (or should have known) was provided with products or services of the Business by the Seller or the Stockholder at any time, for the purpose of offering services or products which compete with or are functionally similar to the services or products of the Business supplied by the Seller or the Stockholder.' the clause becomes overly broad and non specific. It contradicts with section 6 stating: 'It is acknowledged, understood and agreed that the restrictions contained in this Agreement (a) are made for good, valuable and adequate consideration received and to be received by the Stockholder, and (b) are reasonable and necessary, in terms of the time, geographic scope and nature of the restrictions, for the protection of the Buyer and the good will thereof.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is acknowledged, understood and agreed that the restrictions contained in this Agreement (a) are made for good, valuable and adequate consideration received and to be received by the Stockholder, and (b) are reasonable and necessary, in terms of the time, geographic scope and nature of the restrictions, for the protection of the Buyer and the good will thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the 'Restrictive Period' and specificity regarding the solicitation of persons or entities related to the business. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which explicitly states that the restrictions are 'reasonable and necessary, in terms of the time, geographic scope and nature of the restrictions.' By removing the time restriction and the link to the Business, the clause becomes overly broad and potentially unreasonable, creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Stockholder and the Buyer hereby acknowledge and agree that any breach by the Stockholder, directly or indirectly, of the foregoing restrictive covenants will cause the Buyer and/or its affiliates irreparable injury for which there is no adequate remedy at law. Accordingly, the Stockholder expressly agrees that, in the event of any such breach or any threatened breach hereunder by the Stockholder, directly or indirectly, the Buyer and its subsidiaries shall be entitled, in addition to any and all other remedies available, to seek and obtain injunctive and/or other equitable relief to require specific performance of or prevent, restrain and/or enjoin a breach under the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Stockholder and the Buyer hereby acknowledge and agree that any breach by the Stockholder of the foregoing covenants will cause injury. Accordingly, the Stockholder expressly agrees that, in the event of any such breach by the Stockholder, the Buyer and its subsidiaries shall be entitled to remedies.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'directly or indirectly, of the foregoing restrictive covenants will cause the Buyer and/or its affiliates irreparable injury for which there is no adequate remedy at law. in addition to any and all other remedies available, to seek and obtain injunctive and/or other equitable relief to require specific performance of or prevent, restrain and/or enjoin a breach under the provisions of this Agreement.' the type of injury is undefined as well as the remedies that can be given. This contradicts with section 3 stating: 'In the event of any dispute under or arising out of this Agreement, the prevailing party in such dispute shall be entitled to recover from the non-prevailing party or parties, in addition to any damages and/or other relief that may be awarded, its reasonable costs and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) incurred in connection with prosecuting or defending the subject dispute.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event of any dispute under or arising out of this Agreement, the prevailing party in such dispute shall be entitled to recover from the non-prevailing party or parties, in addition to any damages and/or other relief that may be awarded, its reasonable costs and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) incurred in connection with prosecuting or defending the subject dispute.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies 'irreparable injury for which there is no adequate remedy at law' and the right to 'seek and obtain injunctive and/or other equitable relief'. The changed text removes these specifics, only stating 'injury' and 'remedies.' This omission contradicts the contradicted text, which states the prevailing party is entitled to recover 'in addition to any damages and/or other relief that may be awarded, its reasonable costs and expenses'. By removing the initial specifics about irreparable injury and injunctive relief, the changed text creates ambiguity about the scope of available remedies, directly conflicting with the contradicted text's assumption of certain remedies being available and the ability to recover costs in addition to those remedies. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists as defined by Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York. The parties agree to the exclusive jurisdiction and venue of Florida and Georgia state and federal courts with respect to any action between the parties relating to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws. The parties agree to the jurisdiction and venue of courts with respect to any action between the parties relating to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'of the State of New York' and 'the exclusive' and 'Florida and Georgia state and federal' the jurisdiction is left open-ended. This contradicts with section 3 stating: 'In the event of any dispute under or arising out of this Agreement, the prevailing party in such dispute shall be entitled to recover from the non-prevailing party or parties, in addition to any damages and/or other relief that may be awarded, its reasonable costs and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) incurred in connection with prosecuting or defending the subject dispute.' because the courts are not being defined in the contract, making enforcement unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event of any dispute under or arising out of this Agreement, the prevailing party in such dispute shall be entitled to recover from the non-prevailing party or parties, in addition to any damages and/or other relief that may be awarded, its reasonable costs and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) incurred in connection with prosecuting or defending the subject dispute.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specification of 'State of New York' as the governing law and the specific courts 'Florida and Georgia state and federal courts', making the jurisdiction and venue undefined. This directly contradicts the contradicted text which discusses dispute resolution and awarding costs to the prevailing party. Without a clearly defined jurisdiction, it's unclear which courts have the authority to enforce the agreement and award costs, creating a strong in-text contradiction because the enforcement of the agreement is now unclear, which is essential for the contradicted text to be applicable. The 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition applies.
